name: RBI Repo Rate
description: The repo rate is the interest rate at which the RBI lends short-term funds to commercial banks. It is the central bank's main lever for steering inflation and growth, and every other lending rate in the economy takes its cue from it.
source: Reserve Bank of India, Monetary Policy Committee
releaseSchedule: Reviewed roughly every two months by the Monetary Policy Committee; changes only when the MPC votes to change it.

chart.title: The rate-cutting cycle has begun
chart.subtitle: RBI repo rate, %, at each policy review
chart.source: RBI Monetary Policy Committee; illustrative series
